name: Fontaines de Montjuic Circuit Entry
roadSegment: Fontaines de Montjuic / entry onto final circuit near Placa d'Espanya side
estimatedPassageTime: 16:51 / 17:02 expected first and later circuit entry references; official schedule also lists 17:10 at Fontaines de Montjuic
summary: Lower-Montjuic circuit-entry option that may trade less steep racing for better transit and escape possibilities.
whyItMatters: The official schedule marks Fontaines de Montjuic as the entry to the final circuit, making it a navigable landmark for seeing the race before the climb chaos.

access.estimated_route_from_base: Approach from Placa d'Espanya/metro side before closures; exact pedestrian funnels unknown.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: Urban transit is the planning default. Parking near Placa d'Espanya/Montjuic during race closures is unknown and likely constrained.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: A walk-in from Placa d'Espanya may be more realistic than the castle summit, but course-crossing cannot be assumed.
access.road_closure_notes: Unknown; Barcelona municipal traffic order needed.

pros: Official route landmark at the start of the final circuit.
Likely easier post-race escape than the castle climb if Placa d'Espanya approaches remain open.
Potential access to larger urban sidewalks and transit nodes compared with the upper climb.
cons: Riders may be faster and harder to identify than on the climb.
Lower Montjuic can still be heavily controlled because it feeds the final circuit.
Barrier layout may prevent good sightlines or force spectators far from the road.

logistics.parking: Unknown; avoid relying on nearby parking.
logistics.transit: Placa d'Espanya metro/FGC area is nearby in normal conditions; race-day station exits and crowd controls must be verified.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs official Barcelona closure and pedestrian-circulation plan.

crowdRisk: medium-high: lower than the castle climb, but still a final-circuit gateway in a major city.
bathroomFoodProximity: Potentially better than upper Montjuic in normal conditions, but race-day access to cafes/toilets is unknown.
escapeRoute: Walk toward Placa d'Espanya or Poble-sec after official reopening, avoiding any need to cross the active course.
safetyAssessment: Select a broad pedestrian area behind barriers; avoid traffic islands and median positions at the circuit entry.

timing.best_arrival_window: At least 3-4 hours before expected rider passage; earlier if seeking front barrier.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium-high until transit, barriers, and crossings are published.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 2 official page
sources.note: Supports Fontaines de Montjuic as the final-circuit entry and provides official caravan/rider schedule references for the area.

sources.title: TDF trip data official route note
sources.note: Official ASO ArcGIS layer referenced by the app for Stage 2 route geometry; coordinate follows the app's lower-Montjuic route point.

sources.title: Placa d'Espanya and Montjuic access background via Montjuic article
sources.note: Supports the relationship between Montjuic, Placa d'Espanya, and the hill's public spaces; not a closure source.

open_questions: Will Placa d'Espanya-side pedestrian access remain open after the final circuit closes?
Where exactly will barriers allow spectators near Fontaines de Montjuic?
Are nearby metro/FGC exits modified for the Tour crowds?
